descripcion: Extensions and application notes of the global taxonomy for Ecuador. Does not replace temas.json, it complements it.

notas_de_aplicacion.nota: In Ecuador the LOPDP uses the term 'datos sensibles' to refer to what the European GDPR calls 'special categories of data'. Both concepts are equivalent in substance. When tagging LOPDP articles, use the tema sensitive-data.

The LOPDP article attempting to incorporate this concept is poorly drafted. Its use in consulting practice is limited. Prefer the tema sensitive-data for Ecuadorian articles unless the article explicitly refers to the notion of special category as an autonomous concept.

The supervisory authority in Ecuador is the Superintendency of Personal Data Protection (SPDP), created by the LOPDP. Its pronouncements and resolutions are stored in /ec/pronunciamientos/ and /ec/normativa-secundaria/ respectively.

The Ecuadorian LOPDP regulates legal bases in terms similar to the GDPR. Consent has specific formal and evidentiary requirements that differ from the European model in some respects. Always review the corresponding article of the Regulation for implementation details.
